task_completion.description: Verify that the Vital Signs CRF template was uploaded.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a success message indicating the CRF was created/uploaded, or the 'Vital Signs' CRF appearing in the list of available CRFs in the OpenClinica interface.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Vital Signs CRF is assigned to the Baseline Assessment event as required.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Study Event Definition screen for 'Baseline Assessment' showing 'Vital Signs' in the list of assigned CRFs with the 'Required' flag checked or set to 'Yes'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Vital Signs CRF is assigned to the Follow-up Visit event as required.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Study Event Definition screen for 'Follow-up Visit' showing 'Vital Signs' in the list of assigned CRFs with the 'Required' flag checked or set to 'Yes'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Baseline Assessment is scheduled for subject DM-102 on February 5, 2024.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Subject Matrix or Schedule Event screen showing subject 'DM-102' with a 'Baseline Assessment' event scheduled for '05-Feb-2024' or '2024-02-05'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the correct vital signs data was entered for the subject.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the CRF data entry screen for subject DM-102 showing the entered values: Systolic: 135, Diastolic: 88, and Heart Rate: 78.

task_completion.description: Verify that the CRF entry is marked as Complete.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a 'Mark Complete' checkbox being checked during data entry, a success message stating the CRF is complete, or a green checkmark/completed status icon next to the Vital Signs CRF for DM-102 in the Subject Matrix.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the OpenClinica web interface to perform the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for a web browser navigating the OpenClinica application. The agent should not be using terminal commands (like curl or psql) to directly modify the database.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent actually typed the data into the OpenClinica forms.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing the OpenClinica data entry forms being filled out, rather than the agent just writing the final values in a text editor or terminal.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent uploaded the specified file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the browser's file upload dialog or file input field showing '/home/ga/vital_signs_crf.xls' being selected.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Study Identifier: 'DM-TRIAL-2024'. CRF Name: 'Vital Signs'. CRF File Path: '/home/ga/vital_signs_crf.xls'. Target Subject: 'DM-102'. Target Event: 'Baseline Assessment'. Target Event Date: '2024-02-05'. Expected Data Values: Systolic BP = 135, Diastolic BP = 88, Heart Rate = 78.
